channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: The next 100x — 38 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The next 100x

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-03-17 17:38 UTC

OP

Sealed PSA 9 Charizard packs.
Your welcome b. Stay poor

Early replies (first 3)

61991676
>>61991664
What's the fucking point of sealing that pack?? Why would anyone buy it and not open it?? Are you guys stupid?

61991690
>>61991676
The packs are a collectable on their own. Retards with your mindset is what makes it valuable

61991751
Entire PSA-9 market cap of 1999 Base Set sealed booster packs is basically a microcap roughly 600 PSA-9 packs exist and at about $1.5k each that’s ~$900k total market cap

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61992371
>>61991986
This is why jews run the world

61992657
>>61991909
I sold some pokemon cards recently and let me tell you they are NOT low liquidity and even with ebays disgustingly high fees they're very profitable.

61992691
>>61991676
the seal is for authenticity. it's basically saying this is a real pack feel free to crack the seal and ensure the cards are legit. for packs, the psa seal is meant to be cracked. not sure why they grade it though that's kinda autistic.

61992695
>>61991751
Unlimited is trash.
PSA 9 is trash
If you didn’t buy 1st Ed PSA 10 packs at $400 or lower each, it’s over for you.

61992701
>>61991664
based poke investor

61992719
Why do these threads not get deleted immediately? Pokemon cards are not an investment

61992741
>>61991664
Sealed packs are actually a retarddd investment

61992754
>>61992719
we are making money. linkies are not.

61992787
>>61992719
>Pokemon cards are not an investment
They're beating all my investments

61992882
sorry my english is bad but basically pokemon is one giant ponzi scheme like other collectable bubbles it works like this
>((((auction houses)))) and (((((influencers))))) see a hobby or interest that is easy to corner the market with, a rare game or card or whatever BS, either buy up the supply and then once they control the supply, just keep selling it back and forth to themselves or other people in their circle at increasingly higher prices, also see (((private sales))) on instagram or forums where people can just announce that they have sold card at a certain price without any proofs
>now every retard who had 0 interest in these cards despite them being sat for a decade on ebay shops for 1/100th of their current prices are now all piling in, and thus the "pokeinvestor" is born
>also add ridiculous premiums for ((((grading)))), the graders then charge not for just their grading service but you get charged based on the final value of the card, so now all these cards are at retarded hyperinflated prices, it's basically a licence to print money because they charge on the cards perceived worth
>oh also i forgot to add, these large collectible auction houses are run by people who have been fined in the past for manipulating coin markets in the 80s
>oh i forgot to mention that in auction houses TOS they reserve the right to bid on their own item. this means anyone else bidding on the items against the auction houses is instantly at a 20-30% disadvantage because the auction house doesnt have to pay the buyers fee
>add to that all of the other bullshit companies surrounding it and their (((ceos)))), even bullshit like whatnot now has a RICO lol.....

the rabbit hole goes so deep, there's just a whole web of companies involved and this is why now any hobby that was once enjoyable like retro gaming or trading cards is now just a bullshit scam with retards telling you it's an investment

61992898
>>61992882
>the graders then charge not for just their grading service but you get charged based on the final value of the card,
thats only a PSA thing, not every grading company is greedy. yes the big sales are astroturfed but they cant control the market for cards with a massive population

61992984
>>61991751
The market cap of my shit is literally $0. Invest today

61993076
>>61991986
Its a collectable.
I am 99% certain you are a nigger that does not grasp the concept of scarcity and collectibles.

61993140
>>61992741
>Sealed packs are actually a retarddd investment
basically up only for 30 years anon, beats s&p and even nvidia

61993191
>>61993140
you forget the 2003-2019 era where it was the gayest bull shit imaginable

61993439
>>61991981
I dont deal with cards. Pokemon is one of the most succesful IPs of all time though. Even more successful than Star Wars. Its going to last alot longer than you think.

61993586
>>61993191
whilst demand was rock bottom for a long time (maybe till 2016) base set packs definitely cost alot more in 2016 than 2003 so my point still stands

61993629
>>61993439
Agreed, despite everything after gen 4 being a sharp downturn (aside from pokemon go which was instead a severe waste of potential)
But paraphernalia like this only matters to pure nostalgia nerds that were hitting their formative years in the 90s-2000s
Not only are they now hitting middle age but they don't have anywhere near the spare wealth the generations before them had

61993710
>>61993586
its in a mellenial bubble and will phase out of interest. every game ever since red/blue has been red/blue remastered. until they make another good game its not going to generate the same kind of hype ever again. people will get old and sell these cards to pay for bills, except the zoomers of 2030, 2040 ect wont give a fuck about pokemon. unless they make a good ass game

61993733
Bought these two bad boys last week :)

61993815
>>61993710
pretty sure every pokemon game has outsold the previous (mainline) dont fall for the fud, pokemon aint going anywhere its the new disney

61994744
Seller got fomo and refunded me
The hype is real

61994875
>>61994744
kek no way that sucks, you gonna find another?

61994900
>>61994744
that's gay

61994963
>>61994875
>>61994900
Yes it's just one of many orders. I'm stacking these (got about 5) already and just going all in trying to grow my collection like gold bars

61995010
>>61994963
any reason why psa 9 base set packs? wouldnt stacking say blister packs be better? (just as secure + might perform better as "could be heavy" still)

61995153
>>61995010
yeah PSA10 packs are quite hard to come by, theres an abundance of 9 on the market. The PSA 9 graded packs let normies know they are genuine and haven't been resealed or anything dodgy so these will always be seen as 'legit' in a minefield of scams. For that reason the PSA graded packs are very normie friendly, Extra money can be made if you can source genuine sealed packs (there are places, i found some) that go for about 50% off the price of PSA graded ones and send them to PSA yourself for verification

61996073
>>61995153
what about like psa 1-8 or do you only want 9s?

61996103
>>61996073
i'd imagine if a pack is getting any grade below like a 7 then the cards inside are likely bent or scratched in some way. nta but if i were to buy sealed packs i'd probably only buy 8's and 9's

61996175
Who. Fucking. Cares.

item.title: The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack. He is getting closer to 1 million btc — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack. He is getting closer to 1 million btc

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-03-17 17:34 UTC

OP

The GIGACHAD just did it again. 22,337 BTC added to his stack.

He is getting closer to 1 million btc

Early replies (first 3)

61990513
>>61990491
Based chad pumping when everyone is losing

61990575
>>61990491
what a madman, pamp it

61990576
He might become one of the most powerful GOYIM in the world.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61990588
>>61990491
This is not going to end well.

61990633
Who’s going to buy it if he’s buying it all and owns it all?

61990690
>>61990633
He can't own it all because my coins are not for sale.

61990817
>>61990576
The Jews will be forced to bow to Saylor. Truly kino

61990842
all dose satoshis

61990985
>>61990633
he never sell

61990998
And it can all be lost by making one typo in the blockchain address.

61991053
>He is getting closer

Satoshi still holds way more, which he mined for himself almost for free!!!!!!

61992027
>>61990633
He's creating all kinds of products using his btc. The STRC thing is getting huge and at this pace, he will just keep buying

61992128
>>61990633
he will rent it out. you can lease a coin from him at (((fair rates)))

61992161
>>61990491
Does anyone know about the safeguards and plans he installed in case he doesn't wake up one day?

61992164
>>61990633
Greater Eggman theory

61992259
>>61990491
>*buys the local top in yo'ure path*
But yeah, BTC never goes below the ATL before the previous ATH. His strategy just works on the long run.
>>61992161
Do you think he has cold wallets under his bed and he just knows the phrases by heart? The coins are owned by his company, but by him directly.

61993238
>>61992164
he's got the master plan

61993248
His whole buying strategy seems to be to put in a big market order causing the price to spike 3% then hope retail fomo’s in behind him. Hasn’t worked yet.

61993309
>>61990633
He will sell all at market

61993720
>>61990633
>2026
>retards still don’t know how bitcoin works

61993738
>>61993720
Him plus literally 90% of humanity.. And then tell me why we can't just kill them all?

61993818
>>61990491
literally the egg man

61993820
This motherfucker is insane, I wish I knew what was going on in his head.

61993857
>>61992027
Stretch is a line of credit at a floating 11.5% interest(prime plus 8) which is on the upper limit of how bad junk bonds can get. This is the end, there is no new synthetic product coming down the pipe that could possibly be better that STRC. He’ll sell as much as he can until even the dumb money realizes the interest payments don’t pencil out, and then close up shop for good. It might take 2 months, it might take 2 years, but if there’s no money coming in then eventually the whole scheme falls apart.

61993928
>>61993820
drugs and ego

61993947
>>61990491
This man is an expert at buying tops

61993962
>>61990633
He can't buy it all

61995947
>>61992164
I am warning you, delete this post at once.

61995978
>>61990491
Well, he's not buying anymore right now, but it's not like he was even a major name back when we had previous runs.

61995994
>>61995978
he buys more every day, thats the whole point of the STRC ponzi.

Nigggas give him money for STRC, he immediately buys bitcoin with it. A constant TWAP.

if STRC is > $100 he he is buying btc

61996019
>>61995994
You didn't see that he just announced pausing it.

61996165
>>61990588
checked and rekked
the kekkels will be neverending wen retard starts dumping

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 69 replies — 79.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-03-17 16:53 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61968186
>>61966637
>I DOT buy, I DOT care
>i don't sell, i don't care

61968274
This crypto is 1.50.
The fuck?

Not a stock!

61971016
https://polkadothalving.com/ 2 more days

61971138
Prepare yourselves guys. A once in a lifetime event is about to happen. The millennial lunar eclipse is about to happen.

61972032
>>61971138
Locked and loaded fellow DOThead

61972844
>>61932834
This is the pedochain and the total ruin of investors. This crap is just going to go lower, all the way to zero.

61975396
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2031796064085242150
"
Ongoing economic upgrade for Polkadot introduces DOT scarcity in 3 days.

The first Polkadot Halving will deliver a daily reduction of issuance by 53%:
• Maximum supply: 2.1B DOT
• Reduced long-term inflation
• A more predictable monetary model
"

I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.

61976472
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1 more day

61976836
>>61975396
Maybe you should have mentioned this beforehand

61977306
staking like 650 dot on kraken

61978116
>>61976836
Mentioned what?

>>61977306
>staking like 650 dot on kraken
Do it on Hydration or Bifrost. You get peanuts on Kraken compared to those 2. Also, you'll be supporting the coin itself by doing this. Or at least use the official staking dashboard

Bifrost
https://app.bifrost.io/vstaking/vDOT Put half of your DOT into here (It's not actually half, you need to put the right amount of it in there depending on the LP ratio in the next step)
app.bifrost.io/swap/pool/stable/DOT_vDOT?type=add Then put DOT and vDOT in the LP
https://app.bifrost.io/farming?network=polkadot Then put it into the farm

Hydration
https://app.hydration.net/strategies Or use GDOT. This gets you less than Bifrost

Official staking dashboard
https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview This gets you more than hydration but less than Bifrost and you also can't instantly unstake

61980116
https://polkadothalving.com/ 6h 40min

61981693
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9jK-NcRmVcw
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h 40min

61981809
https://polkadothalving.com/ 1h

61981826
>funding rate at -0.1

Sheesh, I've never seen it that low

61983532
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2032546667421909092
"
Polkadot’s issuance model upgrade is scheduled to coincide with Pi Day on March 14.

This protocol change introduces, among others:
‣ A maximum supply of 2.1B DOT, of which ~80% has already been issued.
‣ A DOT emission rate reduction by ~53% on March 14, and further reductions are scheduled over time.

These changes were proposed by the community and ratified by OpenGov as part of an ongoing effort to limit long‑term issuance while maintaining incentives and providing a transparent, predictable issuance schedule.
"

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

item.title: Ripple cucking XRP holders again — 73 replies — 79.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Ripple cucking XRP holders again

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-03-17 16:50 UTC

OP

Oof. Thanks for the money doe

Early replies (first 3)

61970723
>>61970713
>xerp
>creep toes
pick one!

61971052
>>61970713
89383 xrp left

61971076
>>61970713
stick to your containment

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61979223
>>61979017
>out of nowhere
Kek, you'd had to have been living under a rock to not be aware that the majority of xrp seethe comes from mindbroken linkbaggies

61979295
>>61972246
all lies.
Ripple built their business around ledger that can be sold fitted to customer's needs.
XRP is just marketing product and IF someone would use XRPL it would use RLUSD or other stablecoin as gas.

61982361
>>61979295
RLUSD can't be used as gas, XRP is the only counterparty-less asset on ledger that will or can ever exist and is the most efficient and default used for auto-bridging between them, particularly between differently denominated assets, like bonds or stocks or tokenized moneymarkets or MPT tokens or stable dollars or stable yen or stable...etc. Lol You act like the stablecoin was a wrench in our thesis when OUR ENTIRE THESIS was LITERALLY ALWAYS that there would be hundreds of stablecoins and walled gardens and traditional and blockchain ledgers, which is literally the point. Ripple have acquired a broker dealer that processes trillions of dollars annually and is going to use XRPL for post-trade settlement. They are literally building an end-to-end institutional tech stack that uses XRP at every point. Their entire corporate action is basically building a business that brings XRP to corporate finance and function as broadly and deeply focused on liquidity base layers as possible and they have done an extremely good job doing so:
https://ripple.com/insights/institutional-defi-on-xrpl-scaling-real-world-finance-with-xrp-at-the-core/

Even if it makes you mad, this is inarguably the case.

61982372
>>61979017
You are either new or disingenuous. The same 2 or 3 retards make these threads to cope and get BTFO every single time. This was not a new and random mention and has years of precedent. They NEVER know how to defend their attacks or even their own technology, they literally just throw shit into the fan and get shocked and mad and very flustered and confused when it blows back in their face.

61982379
>>61978659
This literally isn't true. Any secondary programmatic sales (which is how they do almost all of their sales) are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings and XRP itself is the LITERAL ONLY other crypto to be declared a non-security officially except for BTC, regardless of what future admin decides. XRP is not a security, certain sales offerings are. Oranges are not securities, certain structured offerings of orange groves can be.

61983504
>>61982379
>Any secondary programmatic sales are implicitly ruled to not be securities offerings
Yes, sales to retail via exchanges (aka programmatic sales) are not securities.

>which is how they do almost all of their sales
Exactly right, all of the XRP sold by Ripple is sold to retail.

This is exactly what I've been saying all along.
Glad you finally saw the light lmao

61983508
>>61982372
>Ripple triple dips by selling both tokens and stock, and then using the token sales to buy back the stock like the scammers they are
>"REEEEE FUCKING LINKIES AAAAA"

lmao

61985136
>>61983504
XRP is itself not a security and they literally haven't sold any to retail, they sell directly with buffer to institutional clients in a demand neutral way. Any crypto offering marketed in a particular way is a security offering. XRP is actually open to more forms of offering with clarity as a non-security because it is federally recognized as not being one (only other coin is bitcoin). Ripple can structure the sale to not be a securities offering VERY easily and have been doinig so throughout the suit over the last several years, but do not sell to retail and account for <1% of total volume which affects price less than a cent literally. Chainlink Labs dump directly on your head directly from node rewards anytime there is a 5% pump.
>>61983508
This is really deep cope. Lol You chose wrong-you are getting a whole nother attempt at choosing and buying with the dump that is happening/coming. Making it is more meaningful than signaling obsessive cult adherence to a fucking crypto project that was LITERALLY built to randomly insert rent seeking fees and solve ZERO problems and improve nothing of Chainlink Labs' products, who subsidize use and largely are used just for price feeds. You don't get anything by entombing yourself permanently in a corny culture of late normie greed fags hoping lightning strikes twice because you are incapable of forecasting trend. All you get is pissed off and carved out to being a really weird unlovable type of NPC. Choose to make it instead.

61985603
>>61985136
>they literally haven't sold any to retail
You just said yourself selling to retail ("programmatic sales") is how they do almost all of their sales.

>they sell directly
They can't. Not without filing a "notice of exempt offering of securities", which they have never done for any sale of XRP.
Probably because filing this document means admitting they're selling securities lol

61985792
>>61972246
>building an end to end tech stack that can establish XRP as the liquidity base of defi and the evolution of tradfi and the bridge between them
this has not been built
you're delusional

61985918
>>61970713
But mcap is 85 billion so they will just instant sell anything they buy for profit

61986807
>>61985792
>t-this has not been built
>y-you're delusional

61987872
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically through exchange through blind bid auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact >>61985792
It literally has. Hidden Road (now Ripple Prime) is a prime broker that handles back end for trillions in settlement annually with special emphasis in digital assets and privileged positioning for its build. GTreasury handles liquidity optimization and treasury management for thousands of companies and many Fortune 500s (bringing their idle capital onchain and moving through it), Metaco/Standard Custody for institutional grade custody used by many of the world's biggest banks already. They have a national bank charter with the OCC. Rail adds virtual accounts for collections and automated fiat-stablecoin conversion/settlement. Palisade gives wallet-as-a-service custody with MPC security, fast provisioning, multi-chain, and treasury automation for scalable fund sweeping. Their platform unifies collection, custody, exchange, settlement, and payout across fiat and digital assets in a single seamless API driven solution. Literal end-to-end solution doing exactly what I said and built piecewise with XRP at the heart of every single piece of it. Their corporate strategy is literally to drive XRP into the deep system of things by building the corporate architecture around it and having others enter it through them. Complete opposite to Chainlink who just shill to retards by publishing pictures of numbers that never translate to use which is only used as a jewish fee insertion for no reason. Pic related.

61987881
>>61985603
Literal retard. They sell programmatically on exchange through blind auctions in a DEMAND NEUTRAL way arranging exact same amount purchases for institutional clients. Their net impact on the exchange is 0. They literally do this already and have throughout the lawsuit while they had the injunction limiting their sales. You are SO a disingenuous, low IQ cope clinging and desperate.

61987900
>>61970713
should I sell my chainlink?
I'm up ~3x still on dca but chainlinkfag keeps losing his mind over xrp and I'm thinking it must be over for link

61987906
>>61987872
>>61987881
Yes, Ripple has been selling XRP exclusively through blind sales via exchanges ("programmatic sales"). In other words: to retail.

This is the third time you're agreeing with me on the same exact thing.
Are you sure you're ok?

61989506
>>61987906
No. Lol This is literally not remotely hard to understand. Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange to them in exact proportion having ZERO impact on demand. For like 9 months this was the fud you spammed and know it "it doesn't increase demand!!!" You are pretending to be a low IQ faggot but you are literally just hopeless without a leg to even beg to stand on in your own thesis or your own fud and are desperate to hope your gay rhetoric will obscure what is easily demonstrable reality. I cannot even imagine being like you. And the fact this is the best you can come up with, collectively as a group, is why you have failed so totally in messaging and convincing people (because your project is a literal founder dump scam) and is a huge vote in direction of XRP as good and link as irredeemable. Link has nothing to do with CLL services and is just an introduction of friction into its process to collect rent. It solves and does nothing. Become a real person again.

61989766
>>61970713
>people familiar with the matter
>under anonymity
Oh so they made it up.

61990596
>>61987872
HAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA

>>61989506
>Ripple set up equivalent bids/asks between themselves and customers and sell through exchange
This is your fourth time agreeing with me that Ripple only sells to retail.

61990673
>ctrl + f "literal"
>43 hits
Certified sperg.

61993148
>>61990673
Certified non-argument haver coping by fully avoiding the substance and hoping rhetoric games about how I am cringe or something for actually being substantial will outweigh the truth mattering. Couldn't be me.

61993164
>>61990596
You are literallly retarded. Ripple Treasury (formerly Gtreasury) routes through and is connected to SWIFT. Nowhere did I or anyone claim SWIFT is part of the stack they built. You zoomed in on something you aren't even right about and ignored the graphic portraying exactly what I claimed to cope. You are currently trying to for the 100th thread after acting like Ripple sales of XRP are securities pretend that directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time and does not impact price at all is "selling to retail." It is so disingenuous and faggoted it is kind of literally unbelievable how low you are willing to diminish yourself rather than just accepting reality. You are past the point of even vaguely reasonable critique and have let yourself become a weird person because you were OBSESSED with a fucking crypto project that you were wrong about. SO insane. I cannot even imagine being like this.

61993236
>>61993164
When will xrp moon? Me want money

61993358
>>61993164
>directing a sale through an exchange in a demand neutral way that organizes a buyer of exactly equivalent size in real time

You're describing how all sales work. You can't sell without buyers "of exactly equivalent size".
You're pic related. You're the meme.

Also, blind sales via exchanges = sales to retail.

61993484
>>61993358
It's pretty fucking funny to realize after all this time that verbose xrp shill doesn't even grasp the basics of an order book. I stopped engaging with him half a year ago and I still feel stupid for having wasted time arguing with this mouth breather. Your patience is legendary anon.

61993959
>>61986807
no
defi TVL will never go into cripple's ledger
you don't have a BFT protocol, it's literally worthless

>>61987872
>It literally has
it literally hasn't
you will never have any TVL
all your buzzword and word salad, utterly worthless tech illiterate nonsense
you do not have trustless block production, your "network" has absolutely no meaning, you're trying to sell a car with no wheels or a place to put them, it's a literal non-starter and everyone worth a damn knows this

enjoy your slow crawl out of the top300

61994433
>>61993358
No, you literal ironic fucking retard, you do not. Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched for their institutional client through the exchange with it to hit the order book at the exact same time, exactly equating the water analogy I mentioned. Insane you are pretending to not understand this.
>>61993959
Something isn't word salad just because you are unable to respond to it and that frustrates you. You are not entitled to automatic rightness and editor of reality fit to render things incoherent which aren't. Ripple already has TVL despite literally being designed to not have L1 smartcontracts etc. Lol attacing us for having byzantine agreement instead of proof of stake/work is not a deep attack and the funniest thing of your reply is you mirroring the terminology I use. You try to start it by having me be dismissed as incoherent and not considered because you can't address my points (conceding you are insecure in your argument/position) and you then supplicate yourself to me mirroring my language use signaling that you understand my competence and wish to try to conform to it and internalize it by imitative association. You can't fake it til you make it here and you demonstrate yourself to be a literal NPC. Enjoy being a literal cult member to a tech that solves no problem, and inserts friction into the broader software solution as a literal jewish rent seeking fee insertion with no purpose beyond this and being sold to you to fund the software company. Just XRP's recent move over the past few days of +11% up is several billion dollars more than link's entire marketcap. Pic related is since the Trump pump.

61995144
>>61994433
>Ripple place a sell order at the same time they have a buy order matched
Yes, that's how selling and buying works.
You're in here pretending that Ripple invented the order book, holy kek.

This is the level of IQ keeping Ripple afloat.

>>61993484
I remember a time when saying something that stupid got you dogpiled on here. Did this guy simply out-retard everyone through attrition, like he did with you?

61995184
>>61972246
the absolute irony of complaining about the middleman while being completely superflous in a world of atomic swaps

or what, do you think institutions will swap their stablecoins with XRP inbetween? lol good thing we have XRP, as we all know, USDT (usd) to EURC (euro) isn't possible with this godlike 8R1DGE CURR3NCY

61996022
>>61995184
Newest amendment enables atomic swaps so yes this would be possible.

item.title: What do I do? — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What do I do?

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-03-17 17:44 UTC

OP

>35 soon
>120k NW
Do I just keep paupermaxxing and dumping all my savings into my brokerage account + maxxing my Roth every year?

I save about 2k a month. I’ve become a bit of a stock picking fag, but I want to know if I should just dump the 2k in VT instead of trying to beat the market. My IQ is about 107 and I’m a lazy and unambitious retard so my only “skill” is pauperism

Early replies (first 3)

61995651
>>61995637
>Roth
>thinking you’ll have the privilege to live to retirement age
Bigger scam than life insurance

61995663
>>61995637
Wait until you have 200k and retire in Latin America/SEA/non-EU Eastern Europe.
>nooo I don't want to live in first world
You said "unambitious" right there. Pick the easy life or continue to struggle the rest of your life, up to you

61995677
>>61995663
That's my plan as well, I thought I would need 300 or 400k though, do you reckon 200k is enough?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61995685
>>61995637
120k is literally nothing

61995716
>>61995637
Market is overinflated. Just buy 5,000 Link for 40k. And your rich by 2028 at most.

61995720
>>61995677
1. Subtract your current age from average life expectancy age
2. Workout an inflation adjusted annual budget based on the lifestyle you want in the country you want to live in
3. Multiply the cost from 2 by the years in 1
4. You now have how much money you’ll need to live there indefinitely, or at least some sort of approximation

61995734
>>61995677
200k is enough for soft retirement, let it stack up whilst you work part time remote or some shit

61995748
>>61995637
Test.

61995750
>>61995637
>just dump the 2k in VT instead of trying to beat the market
Retirement accounts should be in index funds or retirement date funds, especially for midwits.
If you're all ready maxing out your 401k and IRA and have anything left over, you can gamble with that.

61995764
>>61995750
I’m only maxxing out my Roth. ~2k goes in my brokerage

61995783
>>61995750
Usually you want to max out tax advantaged accounts first.
Are you at least getting the full employer match from your 401k?
Are you saving for any big purchases like a house?

61995788
>>61995764
>>61995783
Meant for you

61995900
>>61995734
how is 200k enough to retire in SA wtf that is nothing are u gonna live in the ghetto in some third world shitthole lmao

61995924
>>61995677
200k should be enough to be a poor NEET without a family. If you want to be well off with a family, I'd say even in second world that starts at 500k

61995945
>>61995900
>are u gonna live in the ghetto in some third world shitthole lmao
Better to retire in a cheap shithole than work in an expensive shithole

61996012
>>61995716
Do stinkies really

61996164
>>61995663
Basically my plan, desu. Moving to D.R. or Mexico City in my 40s and done with this shit.

61996187
>>61995716
Silence, jam jeet.

61996197
>>61995900
200k with a two bucket strategy (60% voo, 40% bonds) is 650$ a month, which is above minimum wage in a lot of countries.

item.title: PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-03-17 17:44 UTC

OP

here you go lads, in this canister you can play PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN. A nice side effect of playing PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN is that you get rewarded in ARC token, which can not be bought, only obtained as a reward for playing PACMAN on this canister https://bkysp-nqaaa-aaaam-qh3ia-cai.icp0.io/.
every round of PACMAN costs 0.25 icp, but if anyone rolls trips, i'll lower cost to 0.025 for 24 hours.
there is a leaderboard you can submit your high scores for bragging rights.
the game itself is a buggy mess due to being my first game, also being on a cutting edge technology that is poorly explored, so if you lose money, go fuck yourself!

Early replies (first 3)

61993865
>>61993849
based op

61993869
>>61993849
SAAR!

61993870
>>61993865
ty fren

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61993955
1) Pacman is not being played "on the chain" in the sense that the state is not being uploaded on-chain and validated every frame. The game is loaded from the chain and played in the browser.
2) In the unlikely event that your token ever accrued any value, a pacman-playing bot would take over the leaderboard and destroy that value.

61994004
>>61993955
well duh, making 1000's of updates per second would be too expensive. obviously you put parts of it offchain you brainlet. you could very well do it with rust or even motoko for that matter, it's just not economical.
also the payouts are regressive and based on the total amount of tokens in the reward treasury, meaning you can obtain less and less the later you join.

tl;dr SO FUCKING WHAT?!?! its just PACMAN on the BLOCKCHAIN

61994033
>>61994004
>well duh, making 1000's of updates per second would be too expensive
if there is no on-chain validation, validation is centralized. not only is there is no way to enforce that your game of pacman is being played how you intend, but players themselves cannot verify that they are not being cheated, either by you or by other players exploiting the game. there is no reason to involve a blockchain in this process, since you gain no benefit from using one compared to a centralized server that can at least attempt to filter bots and guarantee that player inputs follow the rules.

61994077
>>61993849
It's nice to see people actually doing stuff on ICP.
Is there somewhere we can see the Pacman game without needing ICP?

61994106
lemme guess... you want me to open that link that looks like it will try to hack me, and then you want me to send you my hard earned money? in exchange i can play pacman that you vibecoded in 10 min? is that the idea?

61994156
>>61994077
the canister is shitting the bed in performance, but here is it anyways

>>61994106
yeah but this way we get to share our scores and play a subpar version of the game TOGETHER

>>61994033
i literally don't care, i had hella fun vibe coding this garbage

61994162
>>61994156
>I don't care how these systems work or what makes them secure or useful
sounds about right yes

61994169
>>61994162
yes

61996018
>>61994004
lol based

61996033
GOT YOUR ASS SHANEONCHAIN

61996133
>>61994169
Actually, anon has a point. if this was a super serious project, you'd want to find a way to prevent users from exploiting the client side game. Not really an issue for a fun play project ofc, but something to think about for the future

61996195
>>61994077
>wowroms

item.title: >BTC going up >silver going down >gold crabbing What the fuck is even going on anymore? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>BTC going up >silver going down >gold crabbing What the fuck is even going on anymore?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-03-17 17:30 UTC

OP

>BTC going up
>silver going down
>gold crabbing
What the fuck is even going on anymore? Everyone told me precious metals would go up with a war with Iran but yet none of that came true. Who the fuck am I supposed to believe now?

Early replies (first 3)

61996045
BTC has been one of the only things going up since the war started. It's unexpected to say the least.

61996094
>>61995845
>op got fooled into thinking big money doesn't diversify
The massive etf inflows weren't a sign or anything
>>61996045
Its only unexpected if you never paid attention to the price action during major state instability habbenings of the last decade

61996115
>>61996045
people fleeing war torn cunts are using crypto to move wealth over borders
if this is unexpected to you in 2026, you should pay more attention

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61996119
>>61996115
How much volume is that really though?
It probably has more to do with ETF flow reversal and Saylor averaging over 8000 BTC a week.

61996141
>>61996045
I heard oil made some gains too

61996148
>>61995845
Gold is actually being sold off, the war is hiding it.
When war end the gold will really plunge.

item.title: Link TA — 8 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Link TA

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-17 17:02 UTC

OP

As we can see here chainlink just briefly eclipsed it’s all time high last cycle in January ‘25 and looks primed to smash through it this year. Fibonacci levels suggest targets between 25x and 60x from current ratios.
Do you think there will be any major news during the coming bullrun for chainlink or will all of the last five years worth of rocket fuel news just quietly start to ignite, forcing a market wide capitulation from air tokens and memes into the operating system of the industry?

item.title: cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices… — 353 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
cope edition >Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices…

Open thread • Replies: 353 • Updated: 2026-03-17 06:42 UTC

OP

cope edition

>Latest developments on the US strikes against Iran
https://iran.liveuamap.com/
https://x.com/sentdefender

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

previously on /smg/

61992015

61992654

61992656
>>61992647

61992659

61992660

61992661

61992662
>>61992647

61992663
>>61992639
I'm still down kek need to be back over 50. But it doesn't matter, this will be worth hundreds soon enough.

61992664
>>61992654

61992665
>>61992651
>>61992652
Now kiss and make up, jeets
We're all good

61992669
>hurr theres ww3 happening but ill long stocks and short oil

61992670
>>61992148
Ex chad here, 3.5 years for me. I have a body count in the high 20s, so my ego doesn't really have anything to prove. My long term gf dumping me kinda just melted my brain. Idk what happened, but I have brutal social anxiety after that. Getting dumped probably made my brain turn off all the serotonin and dopamine levers as some maladaptive survival mechanism

61992672
>>61992654
I bought

61992673
war is bullish, oil shocks are bullish

61992674
Remember when Oil went negative for a couple of days? That was fun.

61992678
>>61992663

61992679
>>61992676
>>61992676

61992680

61992681
>>61992669

61992684
>>61992669

>Look outside
>Suns out, birds are chirping, the sky is blue and the grass is green
>Check monitor
>OIL dumping

61992696

61992720
>President Trump is mulling strikes against key Iranian oil infrastructure on Kharg Island, according to US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz

61992724
>>61992670

61992726
>>61992660

61992871
>>61992678
I literally said it will be 100s lol

61992877
Oil sisters... its over...
We will be oil baggies forever...

61993086
Im officially an oil bagholder. Please laugh at me

61993581
>>61992048
Hope you set a stop loss lol

61993590
>>61992049
Just hold you fucking moron. Have some fucking patience, geez!

61993596
>>61992053

None of those other things really matter on a direct personal level if I make fuckloads of money. Oil dropping to the floor is the entry point I've been waiting for.

61994621
>>61992088

item.title: US economy is finished — 18 replies — 18.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
US economy is finished

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-03-17 16:47 UTC

OP

US economy is finished

Early replies (first 3)

61994908
umm sweaty stock buybacks are bullish

61994911
bullish frfr

61994915
$15 billion is fucking nothing

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61994928
>>61994905
so? Treasure money movements are not market oriented but government expenditure dependent. This just shows tariffs have yielded so much cash for the US to pay off its debt obligations ahead of schedule.
I have little doubt im interpreting this incorrectly.

61995140
>>61994905
>getting out of foreign debt is bad
lol shalom rabbi

61995161
>issue debt at one maturity
>Buyback at a different maturity
Seemsgood

61995167
None of this shit is real anyway

61995376
>>61994905
>$15 billion buyback
>they have been borrowing $50 billion every week for the last three months

I am not sure this is as great as it sounds

61995396
>sold all my municipal bonds
>now black people will never have running water

61995656
>>61995167
This. Its fake money being lent out and fake money used to pay it back.
I cant believe anyone still believes in the monetary system anymore.
If you believe the paper money we use is anything but a meme you are dealing in the real world.
